title: atropa acaulis var. angustifolia
intro: Atropa acaulis var. angustifolia, also known as the wild belladonna or narrow-leaved deadly nightshade, is a plant species belonging to the Solanaceae family. It is a perennial plant with medicinal properties, primarily used for its antispasmodic, anodyne, and sedative effects. The plant is said to have health benefits such as relieving pain, reducing inflammation, and promoting relaxation, although it should be used with caution due to its potential toxicity. Traditionally, Atropa acaulis var. angustifolia has been prepared as a tincture, infusion, or salve, although modern herbalists often recommend more controlled and diluted preparations due to the plant's potency.
